sources.label: BigGo Finance - Nippon Chemi-Con YTD high on AI data center demand, new mid-term plan (Apr 16, 2026 - pre-window, confirms no fresh June catalyst)
Nippon Chemi-Con - Generative AI and aluminum electrolytic capacitors (company AI exposure page)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and only a +0.66% drift day; yesterday's verdict already priced the ~204% YTD rerating, so rank 7 holds. Small negative delta for continued residual-alpha decay: a $0.68B micro-cap that has tripled YTD with no new evidence this week is steadily less hidden and more crowded.
verdicts.bull: Direct bottleneck exposure to AI server VRM power delivery via high-capacity aluminum electrolytic and polymer caps, plus industry-first immersion-cooling-compatible capacitors; the new mid-term plan commits ~¥20B capex to AI data-center share with OPM target of 8%+ by FY3/29 off a 2.9% base, leaving large earnings leverage if execution lands.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully discovered: the April-May mid-term-plan rerating is now in the price, next hard catalyst is the Jul 30 Q1 earnings, and the stock is consolidating rather than offering new information either way.
verdicts.bear: A 204% YTD move in a ~$0.68B name with thin float and passive/momentum crowding has compressed asymmetry; any wobble in AI server build rates or in converting ¥20B capex into margin (legacy OPM was sub-3%) triggers a violent de-rating, and March's share issuance shows dilution is a live funding tool.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Jul 30, 2026 Q1 FY3/27 earnings - first hard read on AI data-center capacitor mix and progress toward the 8%+ OPM mid-term target.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/rerating reversal in a micro-cap that has tripled YTD: execution slippage on capacity ramp or softer AI server orders would unwind the multiple faster than fundamentals support.
